   103→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   104→# Constants
   105→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   106→
   107→_STUB_SCHEMA: dict = {"type": "object", "properties": {}}
   108→
   109→# Skill entry pattern (matches analyzer.py _extract_skill_entries)
   110→_SKILL_ENTRY_RE = re.compile(r"^- ([\w:.-]+): (.+?)$", re.MULTILINE)
   111→
   112→
   113→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   114→# SystemPromptTrimmer
   115→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   116→
   117→class SystemPromptTrimmer:
   118→    """Session-scoped trimmer that reduces system prompt waste.
   119→
   120→    Instantiated once per proxy session. Tracks state across requests:
   121→    - Which tools have been used (for stub/restore logic)
   122→    - Full tool definitions (for re-injection on first use)
   123→    - Component hashes from previous turn (for static detection)
   124→    """
   125→
   126→    def __init__(self, log_fn: Callable[[dict], None] | None = None):
   127→        self.log_fn = log_fn
   128→        # Tool trimming state
   129→        self._used_tools: set[str] = set()
   130→        self._full_tool_defs: dict[str, dict] = {}
   131→        # Static cache state
   132→        self._prev_hashes: dict[str, str] = {}
   133→        # Cumulative stats
   134→        self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved: int = 0
   135→        self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved: int = 0
   136→        self.cumulative_requests: int = 0
   137→
   138→    def trim(self, body: dict) -> TrimResult:
   139→        """Apply all trimming interventions to a request body.
   140→
   141→        Mutates body in place. Returns stats about what was done.
   142→        """
   143→        self.cumulative_requests += 1
   144→        result = TrimResult()
   145→
   146→        # 1. Scan messages for tool usage (before trimming tools)
   147→        self._scan_tool_usage(body.get("messages", []))
   148→
   149→        # 2. Tool definition trimming
   150→        if "tools" in body and isinstance(body["tools"], list):
   151→            result.tools = self._trim_tools(body)
   152→
   153→        # 3. Skill deduplication in message injections
   154→        result.skills = self._dedupe_skills(body.get("messages", []))
   155→
   156→        # 4. Static component tracking (log only, no mutation)
   157→        result.static = self._track_static(body)
   158→
   159→        # Accumulate
   160→        self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved += result.tools.bytes_saved
   161→        self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved += result.skills.bytes_saved
   162→
   163→        # Log trimming decisions
   164→        if self.log_fn is not None:
   165→            self.log_fn({
   166→                "type": "trimming",
   167→                "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   168→                "request_num": self.cumulative_requests,
   169→                "tools": asdict(result.tools),
   170→                "skills": asdict(result.skills),
   171→                "static": asdict(result.static),
   172→                "total_bytes_saved": result.total_bytes_saved,
   173→                "cumulative_tools_saved": self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved,
   174→                "cumulative_skills_saved": self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved,
   175→            })
   176→
   177→        return result
   178→
   179→    def summary(self) -> dict:
   180→        """Current state for the health endpoint."""
   181→        return {
   182→            "requests_trimmed": self.cumulative_requests,
   183→            "tools_used": sorted(self._used_tools),
   184→            "tools_known": len(self._full_tool_defs),
   185→            "cumulative_tools_bytes_saved": self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved,
   186→            "cumulative_skills_bytes_saved": self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved,
   187→        }
   188→
   189→    # --- Internal methods ---
   190→
   191→    def _scan_tool_usage(self, messages: list[dict]) -> None:
   192→        """Scan messages for tool_use blocks to track which tools are used."""
   193→        for msg in messages:
   194→            if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
   195→                continue
   196→            content = msg.get("content", [])
   197→            if not isinstance(content, list):
   198→                continue
   199→            for block in content:
   200→                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use":
   201→                    self._used_tools.add(block.get("name", ""))
   202→
   203→    def _trim_tools(self, body: dict) -> ToolStubStats:
   204→        """Replace unused tool definitions with stubs."""
   205→        tools = body["tools"]
   206→        stats = ToolStubStats(total_tools=len(tools))
   207→        stats.bytes_before = len(json.dumps(tools).encode("utf-8"))
   208→
   209→        # Store full definitions on first encounter
   210→        for tool in tools:
   211→            name = tool.get("name", "")
   212→            if name and name not in self._full_tool_defs:
   213→                self._full_tool_defs[name] = tool.copy()
   214→
   215→        # Build trimmed tool list
   216→        trimmed = []
   217→        for tool in tools:
   218→            name = tool.get("name", "")
   219→            if name in self._used_tools:
   220→                # Restore full definition (ensures we always send the
   221→                # complete schema for tools the model has called)
   222→                full = self._full_tool_defs.get(name, tool)
   223→                trimmed.append(full)
   224→                # Check if this was previously stubbed (restored this turn)
   225→                if tool.get("input_schema") == _STUB_SCHEMA:
   226→                    stats.restored_tools += 1
   227→            else:
   228→                # Stub this tool
   229→                trimmed.append(_make_tool_stub(tool))
   230→                stats.stubbed_tools += 1
   231→
   232→        body["tools"] = trimmed
   233→        stats.bytes_after = len(json.dumps(trimmed).encode("utf-8"))
   234→        return stats
   235→
   236→    def _dedupe_skills(self, messages: list[dict]) -> SkillDedupeStats:
   237→        """Remove duplicate skill entries from system-reminder blocks."""
   238→        stats = SkillDedupeStats()
   239→
   240→        for msg in messages:
   241→            content = msg.get("content", [])
   242→
   243→            if isinstance(content, str):
   244→                if "<system-reminder>" not in content:
   245→                    continue
   246→                before = len(content.encode("utf-8"))
   247→                new_text, entries, dupes = _dedupe_skills_text(content)
   248→                if dupes > 0:
   249→                    msg["content"] = new_text
   250→                    after = len(new_text.encode("utf-8"))
   251→                    stats.bytes_before += before
   252→                    stats.bytes_after += after
   253→                    stats.total_entries += entries
   254→                    stats.duplicates_removed += dupes
   255→                    stats.unique_skills += entries - dupes
   256→                continue
   257→
   258→            if not isinstance(content, list):
   259→                continue
   260→
   261→            for i, block in enumerate(content):
   262→                if not isinstance(block, dict):
   263→                    continue
   264→                text = block.get("text", "")
   265→                if not isinstance(text, str):
   266→                    continue
   267→                if "<system-reminder>" not in text:
   268→                    continue
   269→
   270→                before = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
   271→                new_text, entries, dupes = _dedupe_skills_text(text)
   272→                if dupes > 0:
   273→                    content[i] = {**block, "text": new_text}
   274→                    after = len(new_text.encode("utf-8"))
   275→                    stats.bytes_before += before
   276→                    stats.bytes_after += after
   277→                    stats.total_entries += entries
   278→                    stats.duplicates_removed += dupes
   279→                    stats.unique_skills += entries - dupes
   280→
   281→        return stats
   282→
   283→    def _track_static(self, body: dict) -> StaticCacheStats:
   284→        """Track content hashes of system prompt components across turns."""
   285→        stats = StaticCacheStats()
   286→        current_hashes: dict[str, tuple[str, int]] = {}
   287→
   288→        # System prompt blocks
   289→        system = body.get("system", [])
   290→        if isinstance(system, list):
   291→            for i, block in enumerate(system):
   292→                text = _block_text(block)
   293→                if not text:
   294→                    continue
   295→                h = _content_hash(text)
   296→                size = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
   297→                current_hashes[f"system_{i}"] = (h, size)
   298→        elif isinstance(system, str):
   299→            h = _content_hash(system)
   300→            size = len(system.encode("utf-8"))
   301→            current_hashes["system_0"] = (h, size)
   302→
   303→        # System-reminders in messages
   304→        reminder_idx = 0
   305→        for msg in body.get("messages", []):
   306→            content = msg.get("content", [])
   307→            texts: list[str] = []
   308→            if isinstance(content, str):
   309→                texts = [content]
   310→            elif isinstance(content, list):
   311→                texts = [
   312→                    b.get("text", "")
   313→                    for b in content
   314→                    if isinstance(b, dict) and isinstance(b.get("text"), str)
   315→                ]
   316→            for text in texts:
   317→                for match in re.finditer(
   318→                    r"<system-reminder>(.*?)</system-reminder>",
   319→                    text,
   320→                    re.DOTALL,
   321→                ):
   322→                    inner = match.group(1).strip()
   323→                    h = _content_hash(inner)
   324→                    size = len(match.group(0).encode("utf-8"))
   325→                    current_hashes[f"reminder_{reminder_idx}"] = (h, size)
   326→                    reminder_idx += 1
   327→
   328→        # Compare with previous turn
   329→        stats.total_components = len(current_hashes)
   330→        for key, (h, size) in current_hashes.items():
   331→            prev_h = self._prev_hashes.get(key)
   332→            if prev_h is not None and prev_h == h:
   333→                stats.static_components += 1
   334→                stats.static_bytes_skippable += size
   335→
   336→        # Update for next turn
   337→        self._prev_hashes = {k: h for k, (h, _) in current_hashes.items()}
   338→
   339→        return stats
   340→
   341→
   342→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   343→# Helper functions
   344→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   345→
   346→def _make_tool_stub(tool_def: dict) -> dict:
   347→    """Create a minimal stub for an unused tool definition."""
   348→    desc = tool_def.get("description", "")
   349→    # First line, truncated to keep it compact
   350→    first_line = desc.split("\n")[0].strip()
   351→    if first_line.startswith("- "):
   352→        first_line = first_line[2:]
   353→    if len(first_line) > 120:
   354→        first_line = first_line[:117] + "..."
   355→    return {
   356→        "name": tool_def["name"],
   357→        "description": first_line,
   358→        "input_schema": _STUB_SCHEMA,
   359→    }
   360→
   361→
   362→def _dedupe_skills_text(text: str) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
   363→    """Deduplicate skill entries in system-reminder blocks.
   364→
   365→    Returns (new_text, total_entries, duplicates_removed).
   366→    """
   367→    total_entries = 0
   368→    total_dupes = 0
   369→
   370→    def _replace_reminder(match: re.Match) -> str:
   371→        nonlocal total_entries, total_dupes
   372→        inner = match.group(1)
   373→        if "skills are available" not in inner:
   374→            return match.group(0)
   375→
   376→        lines = inner.split("\n")
   377→        seen_bases: set[str] = set()
   378→        output_lines: list[str] = []
   379→        for line in lines:
   380→            m = _SKILL_ENTRY_RE.match(line)
   381→            if m:
   382→                total_entries += 1
   383→                full_name = m.group(1)
   384→                base = full_name.split(":")[-1] if ":" in full_name else full_name
   385→                if base in seen_bases:
   386→                    total_dupes += 1
   387→                    continue
   388→                seen_bases.add(base)
   389→            output_lines.append(line)
   390→
   391→        return "<system-reminder>" + "\n".join(output_lines) + "</system-reminder>"
   392→
   393→    new_text = re.sub(
   394→        r"<system-reminder>(.*?)</system-reminder>",
   395→        _replace_reminder,
   396→        text,
   397→        flags=re.DOTALL,
   398→    )
   399→    return new_text, total_entries, total_dupes
   400→
   401→
   402→def _block_text(block: dict | str) -> str:
   403→    """Extract text content from a system prompt block."""
   404→    if isinstance(block, str):
   405→        return block
   406→    if isinstance(block, dict):
   407→        return block.get("text", "")
   408→    return ""
   409→
   410→
   411→def _content_hash(text: str) -> str:
   412→    """Short content hash for change detection."""
   413→    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]
   414→
   415→
   416→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   417→# Offline analysis — dataclasses
   418→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   419→
   420→@dataclass
   421→class TrimTurn:
   422→    """Simulated trimming results for one API call."""
   423→
   424→    turn: int
   425→    timestamp: str
   426→    # Tool trimming (estimated from usage patterns)
   427→    tools_used_cumulative: int = 0
   428→    tools_stubbable: int = 0
   429→    tool_bytes_saveable: int = 0
   430→    # Skill dedup (exact)
   431→    skill_entries: int = 0
   432→    skill_duplicates: int = 0
   433→    skill_bytes_saved: int = 0
   434→    # Static tracking (exact)
   435→    total_components: int = 0
   436→    static_components: int = 0
   437→    static_bytes_skippable: int = 0
   438→
   439→
   440→@dataclass
   441→class SessionTrimReport:
   442→    """Aggregate trimming analysis for one proxy log."""
   443→
   444→    log_path: str
   445→    total_turns: int = 0
   446→    total_tools_defined: int = 0
   447→    total_tools_used: int = 0
   448→    per_request_tool_bytes: int = 0
   449→    # Aggregates
   450→    total_skill_duplicates: int = 0
   451→    total_skill_bytes_saved: int = 0
   452→    total_static_bytes_skippable: int = 0
   453→    total_tool_bytes_saveable: int = 0
   454→    turns: list[TrimTurn] = field(default_factory=list)
   455→
   456→    @property
   457→    def avg_skill_bytes_saved(self) -> float:
   458→        if self.total_turns == 0:
   459→            return 0.0
   460→        return self.total_skill_bytes_saved / self.total_turns
   461→
   462→    @property
   463→    def avg_tool_bytes_saveable(self) -> float:
   464→        if self.total_turns == 0:
   465→            return 0.0
   466→        return self.total_tool_bytes_saveable / self.total_turns
   467→
   468→
   469→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   470→# Offline analysis — engine
   471→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   472→
   473→# Approximate bytes for a tool stub entry (name + short desc + minimal schema)
   474→_STUB_BYTES_ESTIMATE = 80
   475→
   476→
   477→def analyze_trimming(path: Path) -> SessionTrimReport:
   478→    """Simulate trimming on a proxy log and report potential savings.
   479→
   480→    Tool definitions are not stored in the log, so tool savings are
   481→    estimated from the inferred per-tool byte overhead and usage counts.
   482→    Skill dedup and static tracking are computed exactly from the logged
   483→    system prompts and messages.
   484→    """
   485→    from pichay.analyzer import (
   486→        _collect_tool_uses,
   487→        _infer_tool_definition_bytes,
   488→        _KNOWN_TOOLS,
   489→    )
   490→
   491→    records = parse_proxy_log(path)
   492→    report = SessionTrimReport(log_path=str(path))
   493→
   494→    requests = [r for r in records if r.get("type") == "request"]
   495→    if not requests:
   496→        return report
   497→
   498→    # Tool usage across the full session
   499→    tool_use_counts = _collect_tool_uses(records)
   500→    tool_def_bytes = _infer_tool_definition_bytes(records)
   501→    report.total_tools_defined = len(_KNOWN_TOOLS)
   502→    report.total_tools_used = len(tool_use_counts)
   503→    report.per_request_tool_bytes = tool_def_bytes
   504→
   505→    # Per-tool byte estimate
   506→    per_tool_bytes = (
   507→        tool_def_bytes / len(_KNOWN_TOOLS) if _KNOWN_TOOLS else 0
   508→    )
   509→
   510→    # Track state across turns (simulating the live trimmer)
   511→    used_tools: set[str] = set()
   512→    prev_hashes: dict[str, str] = {}
   513→
   514→    for turn_idx, req in enumerate(requests):
   515→        messages = req.get("messages_full", [])
   516→        system = req.get("system_prompt_full", [])
   517→
   518→        turn = TrimTurn(
   519→            turn=turn_idx + 1,
   520→            timestamp=req.get("timestamp", ""),
   521→        )
   522→
   523→        # --- Tool trimming estimate ---
   524→        # Scan messages for tool uses up to this point
   525→        for msg in messages:
   526→            if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
   527→                continue
   528→            content = msg.get("content", [])
   529→            if not isinstance(content, list):
   530→                continue
   531→            for block in content:
   532→                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use":
   533→                    used_tools.add(block.get("name", ""))
   534→
   535→        turn.tools_used_cumulative = len(used_tools)
   536→        stubbable = max(0, len(_KNOWN_TOOLS) - len(used_tools))
   537→        turn.tools_stubbable = stubbable
   538→        turn.tool_bytes_saveable = int(
   539→            stubbable * (per_tool_bytes - _STUB_BYTES_ESTIMATE)
   540→        )
   541→
   542→        # --- Skill dedup (exact) ---
   543→        for text in _find_skill_text_blocks(messages):
   544→            _, entries, dupes = _dedupe_skills_text(text)
   545→            if dupes > 0:
   546→                new_text, _, _ = _dedupe_skills_text(text)
   547→                before = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
   548→                after = len(new_text.encode("utf-8"))
   549→                turn.skill_entries += entries
   550→                turn.skill_duplicates += dupes
   551→                turn.skill_bytes_saved += before - after
   552→
   553→        # --- Static tracking (exact) ---
   554→        current_hashes: dict[str, tuple[str, int]] = {}
   555→
   556→        if isinstance(system, list):
   557→            for i, block in enumerate(system):
   558→                text = _block_text(block)
   559→                if not text:
   560→                    continue
   561→                h = _content_hash(text)
   562→                size = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
   563→                current_hashes[f"system_{i}"] = (h, size)
   564→
   565→        reminder_idx = 0
   566→        for msg in messages:
   567→            content = msg.get("content", [])
   568→            texts: list[str] = []
   569→            if isinstance(content, str):
   570→                texts = [content]
   571→            elif isinstance(content, list):
   572→                texts = [
   573→                    b.get("text", "")
   574→                    for b in content
   575→                    if isinstance(b, dict) and isinstance(b.get("text"), str)
   576→                ]
   577→            for text in texts:
   578→                for match in re.finditer(
   579→                    r"<system-reminder>(.*?)</system-reminder>",
   580→                    text,
   581→                    re.DOTALL,
   582→                ):
   583→                    inner = match.group(1).strip()
   584→                    h = _content_hash(inner)
   585→                    size = len(match.group(0).encode("utf-8"))
   586→                    current_hashes[f"reminder_{reminder_idx}"] = (h, size)
   587→                    reminder_idx += 1
   588→
   589→        turn.total_components = len(current_hashes)
   590→        for key, (h, size) in current_hashes.items():
   591→            if prev_hashes.get(key) == h:
   592→                turn.static_components += 1
   593→                turn.static_bytes_skippable += size
   594→
   595→        prev_hashes = {k: h for k, (h, _) in current_hashes.items()}
   596→
   597→        # Accumulate
   598→        report.turns.append(turn)
   599→        report.total_turns += 1
   600→        report.total_skill_duplicates += turn.skill_duplicates
   601→        report.total_skill_bytes_saved += turn.skill_bytes_saved
   602→        report.total_static_bytes_skippable += turn.static_bytes_skippable
   603→        report.total_tool_bytes_saveable += turn.tool_bytes_saveable
   604→
   605→    return report
   606→
   607→
   608→def _find_skill_text_blocks(messages: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
   609→    """Find text blocks containing skill lists in system-reminders."""
   610→    blocks = []
   611→    for msg in messages:
   612→        content = msg.get("content", [])
   613→        if isinstance(content, str):
   614→            if "<system-reminder>" in content and "skills are available" in content:
   615→                blocks.append(content)
   616→            continue
   617→        if not isinstance(content, list):
   618→            continue
   619→        for block in content:
   620→            if not isinstance(block, dict):
   621→                continue
   622→            text = block.get("text", "")
   623→            if not isinstance(text, str):
   624→                continue
   625→            if "<system-reminder>" in text and "skills are available" in text:
   626→                blocks.append(text)
   627→    return blocks
   628→
   629→
   630→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   631→# Display
   632→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   633→
   634→def print_trim_report(r: SessionTrimReport) -> None:
   635→    """Print human-readable trimming analysis."""
   636→    print(f"\n{'='*65}")
   637→    print("SYSTEM PROMPT TRIMMING ANALYSIS")
   638→    print(f"{'='*65}")
   639→    print(f"Log: {r.log_path}")
   640→    print(f"API calls: {r.total_turns}")
   641→
   642→    # Tool trimming
   643→    print(f"\n--- Tool Definition Trimming ---")
   644→    print(f"  Tools defined:     {r.total_tools_defined:>10,}")
   645→    print(f"  Tools used:        {r.total_tools_used:>10,}")
   646→    print(f"  Tool bytes/req:    {r.per_request_tool_bytes:>10,}")
   647→    print(f"  Est. total saved:  {r.total_tool_bytes_saveable:>10,} bytes")
   648→    print(f"  Est. avg saved:    {r.avg_tool_bytes_saveable:>10,.0f} bytes/req")
   649→
   650→    # Skill dedup
   651→    print(f"\n--- Skill Deduplication ---")
   652→    print(f"  Total duplicates:  {r.total_skill_duplicates:>10,}")
   653→    print(f"  Total bytes saved: {r.total_skill_bytes_saved:>10,}")
   654→    print(f"  Avg bytes saved:   {r.avg_skill_bytes_saved:>10,.0f} bytes/req")
   655→
   656→    # Static caching
   657→    print(f"\n--- Static Component Caching ---")
   658→    print(f"  Total skippable:   {r.total_static_bytes_skippable:>10,} bytes")
   659→
   660→    # Per-turn detail
   661→    if r.turns:
   662→        print(f"\nPer-turn:")
   663→        print(
   664→            f"  {'Turn':>4s}  {'Stubs':>5s}  {'ToolSave':>10s}  "
   665→            f"{'SkDupes':>7s}  {'SkSave':>8s}  "
   666→            f"{'Static':>6s}  {'StaticSave':>10s}"
   667→        )
   668→        for t in r.turns:
   669→            print(
   670→                f"  T{t.turn:>3d}  {t.tools_stubbable:>5d}  "
   671→                f"{t.tool_bytes_saveable:>10,}  "
   672→                f"{t.skill_duplicates:>7d}  {t.skill_bytes_saved:>8,}  "
   673→                f"{t.static_components:>6d}  "
   674→                f"{t.static_bytes_skippable:>10,}"
   675→            )
   676→
   677→    # Summary
   678→    total_potential = (
   679→        r.total_tool_bytes_saveable
   680→        + r.total_skill_bytes_saved
   681→        + r.total_static_bytes_skippable
   682→    )
   683→    print(f"\n--- Summary ---")
   684→    print(f"  Tool stub savings:     {r.total_tool_bytes_saveable:>12,} bytes")
   685→    print(f"  Skill dedup savings:   {r.total_skill_bytes_saved:>12,} bytes")
   686→    print(f"  Static cache savings:  {r.total_static_bytes_skippable:>12,} bytes")
   687→    print(f"  Total potential:       {total_potential:>12,} bytes")
   688→
   689→
   690→def _pct(part: int, whole: int) -> str:
   691→    if whole == 0:
   692→        return "0.0%"
   693→    return f"{part / whole:.1%}"
   694→
   695→
   696→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   697→# CLI
   698→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   699→
   700→def main():
   701→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   702→        description="Analyze system prompt trimming potential in proxy logs"
   703→    )
   704→    parser.add_argument(
   705→        "logs",
   706→        type=Path,
   707→        nargs="+",
   708→        help="Proxy JSONL log file(s) to analyze",
   709→    )
   710→    parser.add_argument(
   711→        "--json",
   712→        action="store_true",
   713→        help="Output as JSON",
   714→    )
   715→    args = parser.parse_args()
   716→
   717→    reports: list[SessionTrimReport] = []
   718→    for log_path in args.logs:
   719→        if not log_path.exists():
   720→            print(
   721→                f"Warning: {log_path} not found, skipping.",
   722→                file=sys.stderr,
   723→            )
   724→            continue
   725→        reports.append(analyze_trimming(log_path))
   726→
   727→    if args.json:
   728→        for r in reports:
   729→            out = asdict(r)
   730→            out["turn_count"] = len(out.pop("turns"))
   731→            out["avg_skill_bytes_saved"] = r.avg_skill_bytes_saved
   732→            out["avg_tool_bytes_saveable"] = r.avg_tool_bytes_saveable
   733→            print(json.dumps(out))
   734→        return
   735→
   736→    for r in reports:
   737→        print_trim_report(r)
   738→
   739→
   740→if __name__ == "__main__":
   741→    main()
   742→

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""System prompt trimmer for the proxy.

Three interventions to reduce system prompt waste:

1. Tool definition trimming: Replace unused tool schemas with one-line
   stubs. Re-inject the full definition on first use. The API sends
   ~18 tool definitions per request but only ~5 are used per session —
   45K bytes/request wasted on unused schemas.

2. Skill deduplication: Skills appear tripled under prefixes like
   'pptx', 'example-skills:pptx', 'document-skills:pptx'. Keep only
   the first occurrence of each base skill name.

3. Static component caching: Track content hashes of system prompt
   components across turns. Log which are static and how many bytes
   would be saved (no stripping yet — needs KV cache integration).

Usage:
    # Offline analysis of proxy logs
    python -m pichay.trimmer logs/proxy_*.jsonl

    # With JSON output
    python -m pichay.trimmer --json logs/proxy_*.jsonl
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import json
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Callable

from pichay.eval import parse_proxy_log


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dataclasses — live trimming stats
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class ToolStubStats:
    """Stats from tool definition trimming on one request."""

    total_tools: int = 0
    stubbed_tools: int = 0
    restored_tools: int = 0
    bytes_before: int = 0
    bytes_after: int = 0

    @property
    def bytes_saved(self) -> int:
        return self.bytes_before - self.bytes_after


@dataclass
class SkillDedupeStats:
    """Stats from skill deduplication on one request."""

    total_entries: int = 0
    unique_skills: int = 0
    duplicates_removed: int = 0
    bytes_before: int = 0
    bytes_after: int = 0

    @property
    def bytes_saved(self) -> int:
        return self.bytes_before - self.bytes_after


@dataclass
class StaticCacheStats:
    """Stats from static component hash tracking on one request."""

    total_components: int = 0
    static_components: int = 0
    static_bytes_skippable: int = 0


@dataclass
class TrimResult:
    """Combined result of all trimming interventions on one request."""

    tools: ToolStubStats = field(default_factory=ToolStubStats)
    skills: SkillDedupeStats = field(default_factory=SkillDedupeStats)
    static: StaticCacheStats = field(default_factory=StaticCacheStats)

    @property
    def total_bytes_saved(self) -> int:
        return self.tools.bytes_saved + self.skills.bytes_saved

    @property
    def total_bytes_skippable(self) -> int:
        """Bytes that could be saved with KV cache integration."""
        return self.total_bytes_saved + self.static.static_bytes_skippable


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_STUB_SCHEMA: dict = {"type": "object", "properties": {}}

# Skill entry pattern (matches analyzer.py _extract_skill_entries)
_SKILL_ENTRY_RE = re.compile(r"^- ([\w:.-]+): (.+?)$", re.MULTILINE)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SystemPromptTrimmer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class SystemPromptTrimmer:
    """Session-scoped trimmer that reduces system prompt waste.

    Instantiated once per proxy session. Tracks state across requests:
    - Which tools have been used (for stub/restore logic)
    - Full tool definitions (for re-injection on first use)
    - Component hashes from previous turn (for static detection)
    """

    def __init__(self, log_fn: Callable[[dict], None] | None = None):
        self.log_fn = log_fn
        # Tool trimming state
        self._used_tools: set[str] = set()
        self._full_tool_defs: dict[str, dict] = {}
        # Static cache state
        self._prev_hashes: dict[str, str] = {}
        # Cumulative stats
        self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved: int = 0
        self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved: int = 0
        self.cumulative_requests: int = 0

    def trim(self, body: dict) -> TrimResult:
        """Apply all trimming interventions to a request body.

        Mutates body in place. Returns stats about what was done.
        """
        self.cumulative_requests += 1
        result = TrimResult()

        # 1. Scan messages for tool usage (before trimming tools)
        self._scan_tool_usage(body.get("messages", []))

        # 2. Tool definition trimming
        if "tools" in body and isinstance(body["tools"], list):
            result.tools = self._trim_tools(body)

        # 3. Skill deduplication in message injections
        result.skills = self._dedupe_skills(body.get("messages", []))

        # 4. Static component tracking (log only, no mutation)
        result.static = self._track_static(body)

        # Accumulate
        self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved += result.tools.bytes_saved
        self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved += result.skills.bytes_saved

        # Log trimming decisions
        if self.log_fn is not None:
            self.log_fn({
                "type": "trimming",
                "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
                "request_num": self.cumulative_requests,
                "tools": asdict(result.tools),
                "skills": asdict(result.skills),
                "static": asdict(result.static),
                "total_bytes_saved": result.total_bytes_saved,
                "cumulative_tools_saved": self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved,
                "cumulative_skills_saved": self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved,
            })

        return result

    def summary(self) -> dict:
        """Current state for the health endpoint."""
        return {
            "requests_trimmed": self.cumulative_requests,
            "tools_used": sorted(self._used_tools),
            "tools_known": len(self._full_tool_defs),
            "cumulative_tools_bytes_saved": self.cumulative_tools_bytes_saved,
            "cumulative_skills_bytes_saved": self.cumulative_skills_bytes_saved,
        }

    # --- Internal methods ---

    def _scan_tool_usage(self, messages: list[dict]) -> None:
        """Scan messages for tool_use blocks to track which tools are used."""
        for msg in messages:
            if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
                continue
            content = msg.get("content", [])
            if not isinstance(content, list):
                continue
            for block in content:
                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use":
                    self._used_tools.add(block.get("name", ""))

    def _trim_tools(self, body: dict) -> ToolStubStats:
        """Replace unused tool definitions with stubs."""
        tools = body["tools"]
        stats = ToolStubStats(total_tools=len(tools))
        stats.bytes_before = len(json.dumps(tools).encode("utf-8"))

        # Store full definitions on first encounter
        for tool in tools:
            name = tool.get("name", "")
            if name and name not in self._full_tool_defs:
                self._full_tool_defs[name] = tool.copy()

        # Build trimmed tool list
        trimmed = []
        for tool in tools:
            name = tool.get("name", "")
            if name in self._used_tools:
                # Restore full definition (ensures we always send the
                # complete schema for tools the model has called)
                full = self._full_tool_defs.get(name, tool)
                trimmed.append(full)
                # Check if this was previously stubbed (restored this turn)
                if tool.get("input_schema") == _STUB_SCHEMA:
                    stats.restored_tools += 1
            else:
                # Stub this tool
                trimmed.append(_make_tool_stub(tool))
                stats.stubbed_tools += 1

        body["tools"] = trimmed
        stats.bytes_after = len(json.dumps(trimmed).encode("utf-8"))
        return stats

    def _dedupe_skills(self, messages: list[dict]) -> SkillDedupeStats:
        """Remove duplicate skill entries from system-reminder blocks."""
        stats = SkillDedupeStats()

        for msg in messages:
            content = msg.get("content", [])

            if isinstance(content, str):
                if "<system-reminder>" not in content:
                    continue
                before = len(content.encode("utf-8"))
                new_text, entries, dupes = _dedupe_skills_text(content)
                if dupes > 0:
                    msg["content"] = new_text
                    after = len(new_text.encode("utf-8"))
                    stats.bytes_before += before
                    stats.bytes_after += after
                    stats.total_entries += entries
                    stats.duplicates_removed += dupes
                    stats.unique_skills += entries - dupes
                continue

            if not isinstance(content, list):
                continue

            for i, block in enumerate(content):
                if not isinstance(block, dict):
                    continue
                text = block.get("text", "")
                if not isinstance(text, str):
                    continue
                if "<system-reminder>" not in text:
                    continue

                before = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
                new_text, entries, dupes = _dedupe_skills_text(text)
                if dupes > 0:
                    content[i] = {**block, "text": new_text}
                    after = len(new_text.encode("utf-8"))
                    stats.bytes_before += before
                    stats.bytes_after += after
                    stats.total_entries += entries
                    stats.duplicates_removed += dupes
                    stats.unique_skills += entries - dupes

        return stats

    def _track_static(self, body: dict) -> StaticCacheStats:
        """Track content hashes of system prompt components across turns."""
        stats = StaticCacheStats()
        current_hashes: dict[str, tuple[str, int]] = {}

        # System prompt blocks
        system = body.get("system", [])
        if isinstance(system, list):
            for i, block in enumerate(system):
                text = _block_text(block)
                if not text:
                    continue
                h = _content_hash(text)
                size = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
                current_hashes[f"system_{i}"] = (h, size)
        elif isinstance(system, str):
            h = _content_hash(system)
            size = len(system.encode("utf-8"))
            current_hashes["system_0"] = (h, size)

        # System-reminders in messages
        reminder_idx = 0
        for msg in body.get("messages", []):
            content = msg.get("content", [])
            texts: list[str] = []
            if isinstance(content, str):
                texts = [content]
            elif isinstance(content, list):
                texts = [
                    b.get("text", "")
                    for b in content
                    if isinstance(b, dict) and isinstance(b.get("text"), str)
                ]
            for text in texts:
                for match in re.finditer(
                    r"<system-reminder>(.*?)</system-reminder>",
                    text,
                    re.DOTALL,
                ):
                    inner = match.group(1).strip()
                    h = _content_hash(inner)
                    size = len(match.group(0).encode("utf-8"))
                    current_hashes[f"reminder_{reminder_idx}"] = (h, size)
                    reminder_idx += 1

        # Compare with previous turn
        stats.total_components = len(current_hashes)
        for key, (h, size) in current_hashes.items():
            prev_h = self._prev_hashes.get(key)
            if prev_h is not None and prev_h == h:
                stats.static_components += 1
                stats.static_bytes_skippable += size

        # Update for next turn
        self._prev_hashes = {k: h for k, (h, _) in current_hashes.items()}

        return stats


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper functions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_tool_stub(tool_def: dict) -> dict:
    """Create a minimal stub for an unused tool definition."""
    desc = tool_def.get("description", "")
    # First line, truncated to keep it compact
    first_line = desc.split("\n")[0].strip()
    if first_line.startswith("- "):
        first_line = first_line[2:]
    if len(first_line) > 120:
        first_line = first_line[:117] + "..."
    return {
        "name": tool_def["name"],
        "description": first_line,
        "input_schema": _STUB_SCHEMA,
    }


def _dedupe_skills_text(text: str) -> tuple[str, int, int]:
    """Deduplicate skill entries in system-reminder blocks.

    Returns (new_text, total_entries, duplicates_removed).
    """
    total_entries = 0
    total_dupes = 0

    def _replace_reminder(match: re.Match) -> str:
        nonlocal total_entries, total_dupes
        inner = match.group(1)
        if "skills are available" not in inner:
            return match.group(0)

        lines = inner.split("\n")
        seen_bases: set[str] = set()
        output_lines: list[str] = []
        for line in lines:
            m = _SKILL_ENTRY_RE.match(line)
            if m:
                total_entries += 1
                full_name = m.group(1)
                base = full_name.split(":")[-1] if ":" in full_name else full_name
                if base in seen_bases:
                    total_dupes += 1
                    continue
                seen_bases.add(base)
            output_lines.append(line)

        return "<system-reminder>" + "\n".join(output_lines) + "</system-reminder>"

    new_text = re.sub(
        r"<system-reminder>(.*?)</system-reminder>",
        _replace_reminder,
        text,
        flags=re.DOTALL,
    )
    return new_text, total_entries, total_dupes


def _block_text(block: dict | str) -> str:
    """Extract text content from a system prompt block."""
    if isinstance(block, str):
        return block
    if isinstance(block, dict):
        return block.get("text", "")
    return ""


def _content_hash(text: str) -> str:
    """Short content hash for change detection."""
    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()[:16]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Offline analysis — dataclasses
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@dataclass
class TrimTurn:
    """Simulated trimming results for one API call."""

    turn: int
    timestamp: str
    # Tool trimming (estimated from usage patterns)
    tools_used_cumulative: int = 0
    tools_stubbable: int = 0
    tool_bytes_saveable: int = 0
    # Skill dedup (exact)
    skill_entries: int = 0
    skill_duplicates: int = 0
    skill_bytes_saved: int = 0
    # Static tracking (exact)
    total_components: int = 0
    static_components: int = 0
    static_bytes_skippable: int = 0


@dataclass
class SessionTrimReport:
    """Aggregate trimming analysis for one proxy log."""

    log_path: str
    total_turns: int = 0
    total_tools_defined: int = 0
    total_tools_used: int = 0
    per_request_tool_bytes: int = 0
    # Aggregates
    total_skill_duplicates: int = 0
    total_skill_bytes_saved: int = 0
    total_static_bytes_skippable: int = 0
    total_tool_bytes_saveable: int = 0
    turns: list[TrimTurn] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def avg_skill_bytes_saved(self) -> float:
        if self.total_turns == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.total_skill_bytes_saved / self.total_turns

    @property
    def avg_tool_bytes_saveable(self) -> float:
        if self.total_turns == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.total_tool_bytes_saveable / self.total_turns


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Offline analysis — engine
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Approximate bytes for a tool stub entry (name + short desc + minimal schema)
_STUB_BYTES_ESTIMATE = 80


def analyze_trimming(path: Path) -> SessionTrimReport:
    """Simulate trimming on a proxy log and report potential savings.

    Tool definitions are not stored in the log, so tool savings are
    estimated from the inferred per-tool byte overhead and usage counts.
    Skill dedup and static tracking are computed exactly from the logged
    system prompts and messages.
    """
    from pichay.analyzer import (
        _collect_tool_uses,
        _infer_tool_definition_bytes,
        _KNOWN_TOOLS,
    )

    records = parse_proxy_log(path)
    report = SessionTrimReport(log_path=str(path))

    requests = [r for r in records if r.get("type") == "request"]
    if not requests:
        return report

    # Tool usage across the full session
    tool_use_counts = _collect_tool_uses(records)
    tool_def_bytes = _infer_tool_definition_bytes(records)
    report.total_tools_defined = len(_KNOWN_TOOLS)
    report.total_tools_used = len(tool_use_counts)
    report.per_request_tool_bytes = tool_def_bytes

    # Per-tool byte estimate
    per_tool_bytes = (
        tool_def_bytes / len(_KNOWN_TOOLS) if _KNOWN_TOOLS else 0
    )

    # Track state across turns (simulating the live trimmer)
    used_tools: set[str] = set()
    prev_hashes: dict[str, str] = {}

    for turn_idx, req in enumerate(requests):
        messages = req.get("messages_full", [])
        system = req.get("system_prompt_full", [])

        turn = TrimTurn(
            turn=turn_idx + 1,
            timestamp=req.get("timestamp", ""),
        )

        # --- Tool trimming estimate ---
        # Scan messages for tool uses up to this point
        for msg in messages:
            if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
                continue
            content = msg.get("content", [])
            if not isinstance(content, list):
                continue
            for block in content:
                if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_use":
                    used_tools.add(block.get("name", ""))

        turn.tools_used_cumulative = len(used_tools)
        stubbable = max(0, len(_KNOWN_TOOLS) - len(used_tools))
        turn.tools_stubbable = stubbable
        turn.tool_bytes_saveable = int(
            stubbable * (per_tool_bytes - _STUB_BYTES_ESTIMATE)
        )

        # --- Skill dedup (exact) ---
        for text in _find_skill_text_blocks(messages):
            _, entries, dupes = _dedupe_skills_text(text)
            if dupes > 0:
                new_text, _, _ = _dedupe_skills_text(text)
                before = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
                after = len(new_text.encode("utf-8"))
                turn.skill_entries += entries
                turn.skill_duplicates += dupes
                turn.skill_bytes_saved += before - after

        # --- Static tracking (exact) ---
        current_hashes: dict[str, tuple[str, int]] = {}

        if isinstance(system, list):
            for i, block in enumerate(system):
                text = _block_text(block)
                if not text:
                    continue
                h = _content_hash(text)
                size = len(text.encode("utf-8"))
                current_hashes[f"system_{i}"] = (h, size)

        reminder_idx = 0
        for msg in messages:
            content = msg.get("content", [])
            texts: list[str] = []
            if isinstance(content, str):
                texts = [content]
            elif isinstance(content, list):
                texts = [
                    b.get("text", "")
                    for b in content
                    if isinstance(b, dict) and isinstance(b.get("text"), str)
                ]
            for text in texts:
                for match in re.finditer(
                    r"<system-reminder>(.*?)</system-reminder>",
                    text,
                    re.DOTALL,
                ):
                    inner = match.group(1).strip()
                    h = _content_hash(inner)
                    size = len(match.group(0).encode("utf-8"))
                    current_hashes[f"reminder_{reminder_idx}"] = (h, size)
                    reminder_idx += 1

        turn.total_components = len(current_hashes)
        for key, (h, size) in current_hashes.items():
            if prev_hashes.get(key) == h:
                turn.static_components += 1
                turn.static_bytes_skippable += size

        prev_hashes = {k: h for k, (h, _) in current_hashes.items()}

        # Accumulate
        report.turns.append(turn)
        report.total_turns += 1
        report.total_skill_duplicates += turn.skill_duplicates
        report.total_skill_bytes_saved += turn.skill_bytes_saved
        report.total_static_bytes_skippable += turn.static_bytes_skippable
        report.total_tool_bytes_saveable += turn.tool_bytes_saveable

    return report


def _find_skill_text_blocks(messages: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
    """Find text blocks containing skill lists in system-reminders."""
    blocks = []
    for msg in messages:
        content = msg.get("content", [])
        if isinstance(content, str):
            if "<system-reminder>" in content and "skills are available" in content:
                blocks.append(content)
            continue
        if not isinstance(content, list):
            continue
        for block in content:
            if not isinstance(block, dict):
                continue
            text = block.get("text", "")
            if not isinstance(text, str):
                continue
            if "<system-reminder>" in text and "skills are available" in text:
                blocks.append(text)
    return blocks


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Display
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def print_trim_report(r: SessionTrimReport) -> None:
    """Print human-readable trimming analysis."""
    print(f"\n{'='*65}")
    print("SYSTEM PROMPT TRIMMING ANALYSIS")
    print(f"{'='*65}")
    print(f"Log: {r.log_path}")
    print(f"API calls: {r.total_turns}")

    # Tool trimming
    print(f"\n--- Tool Definition Trimming ---")
    print(f"  Tools defined:     {r.total_tools_defined:>10,}")
    print(f"  Tools used:        {r.total_tools_used:>10,}")
    print(f"  Tool bytes/req:    {r.per_request_tool_bytes:>10,}")
    print(f"  Est. total saved:  {r.total_tool_bytes_saveable:>10,} bytes")
    print(f"  Est. avg saved:    {r.avg_tool_bytes_saveable:>10,.0f} bytes/req")

    # Skill dedup
    print(f"\n--- Skill Deduplication ---")
    print(f"  Total duplicates:  {r.total_skill_duplicates:>10,}")
    print(f"  Total bytes saved: {r.total_skill_bytes_saved:>10,}")
    print(f"  Avg bytes saved:   {r.avg_skill_bytes_saved:>10,.0f} bytes/req")

    # Static caching
    print(f"\n--- Static Component Caching ---")
    print(f"  Total skippable:   {r.total_static_bytes_skippable:>10,} bytes")

    # Per-turn detail
    if r.turns:
        print(f"\nPer-turn:")
        print(
            f"  {'Turn':>4s}  {'Stubs':>5s}  {'ToolSave':>10s}  "
            f"{'SkDupes':>7s}  {'SkSave':>8s}  "
            f"{'Static':>6s}  {'StaticSave':>10s}"
        )
        for t in r.turns:
            print(
                f"  T{t.turn:>3d}  {t.tools_stubbable:>5d}  "
                f"{t.tool_bytes_saveable:>10,}  "
                f"{t.skill_duplicates:>7d}  {t.skill_bytes_saved:>8,}  "
                f"{t.static_components:>6d}  "
                f"{t.static_bytes_skippable:>10,}"
            )

    # Summary
    total_potential = (
        r.total_tool_bytes_saveable
        + r.total_skill_bytes_saved
        + r.total_static_bytes_skippable
    )
    print(f"\n--- Summary ---")
    print(f"  Tool stub savings:     {r.total_tool_bytes_saveable:>12,} bytes")
    print(f"  Skill dedup savings:   {r.total_skill_bytes_saved:>12,} bytes")
    print(f"  Static cache savings:  {r.total_static_bytes_skippable:>12,} bytes")
    print(f"  Total potential:       {total_potential:>12,} bytes")


def _pct(part: int, whole: int) -> str:
    if whole == 0:
        return "0.0%"
    return f"{part / whole:.1%}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CLI
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Analyze system prompt trimming potential in proxy logs"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "logs",
        type=Path,
        nargs="+",
        help="Proxy JSONL log file(s) to analyze",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--json",
        action="store_true",
        help="Output as JSON",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    reports: list[SessionTrimReport] = []
    for log_path in args.logs:
        if not log_path.exists():
            print(
                f"Warning: {log_path} not found, skipping.",
                file=sys.stderr,
            )
            continue
        reports.append(analyze_trimming(log_path))

    if args.json:
        for r in reports:
            out = asdict(r)
            out["turn_count"] = len(out.pop("turns"))
            out["avg_skill_bytes_saved"] = r.avg_skill_bytes_saved
            out["avg_tool_bytes_saveable"] = r.avg_tool_bytes_saveable
            print(json.dumps(out))
        return

    for r in reports:
        print_trim_report(r)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

1→"""Tests for pichay.replay — offline replay of context paging on proxy logs."""

     3→from __future__ import annotations

    23→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→# Helpers
    25→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→def _write_jsonl(tmp_path: Path, name: str, records: list[dict]) -> Path:
    28→    """Write a list of dicts as JSONL and return the path."""
    29→    p = tmp_path / name
    30→    with open(p, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    31→        for rec in records:
    32→            f.write(json.dumps(rec) + "\n")
    33→    return p
    34→
    35→
    36→def _tool_use(tid: str, name: str, inp: dict) -> dict:
    37→    return {"type": "tool_use", "id": tid, "name": name, "input": inp}
    38→
    39→
    40→def _tool_result(tid: str, content: str | list, is_error: bool = False) -> dict:
    41→    d: dict = {"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": tid, "content": content}
    42→    if is_error:
    43→        d["is_error"] = True
    44→    return d
    45→
    46→
    47→def _req(messages_full: list[dict], ts: str = "2026-03-01T12:00:00Z") -> dict:
    48→    return {"type": "request", "timestamp": ts, "messages_full": messages_full}
    49→
    50→
    51→# Content large enough to exceed min_size=500 default.
    52→BIG = "A" * 800
    53→
    54→# Content too small to be evicted with default min_size=500.
    55→SMALL = "x" * 50
    56→
    57→
    58→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→# (1) Empty log file
    60→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61→
    62→class TestEmptyLog:
    63→    def test_returns_zero_turns(self, tmp_path):
    64→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
    65→        s = replay_session(p)
    66→        assert s.total_turns == 0
    67→        assert s.total_evictions == 0
    68→        assert s.total_bytes_saved == 0
    69→        assert s.total_bytes_original == 0
    70→        assert s.total_faults == 0

    72→
    73→    def test_properties_safe_on_empty(self, tmp_path):
    74→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
    75→        s = replay_session(p)
    76→        assert s.fault_rate == 0.0
    77→        assert s.reduction_pct == 0.0
    78→

    80→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
    81→        s = replay_session(p)
    82→        assert s.log_path == str(p)

    84→
    85→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→# (2) Log with no messages_full field (pre-replay logs)
    87→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→
    89→class TestNoMessagesFull:
    90→    def test_pre_replay_log_skipped(self, tmp_path):
    91→        """Older proxy logs that lack messages_full produce no turns."""
    92→        records = [
    93→            {
    94→                "type": "request",

    96→                "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
    97→                "messages": {"tool_result_count": 5},
    98→            },
    99→            {
   100→                "type": "response",

   102→                "usage": {"input_tokens": 1000},

   104→        ]

   109→
   110→    def test_non_request_records_ignored(self, tmp_path):
   111→        """Response, compaction, and other record types are not processed."""
   112→        records = [
   113→            {"type": "response", "timestamp": "T1", "messages_full": []},
   114→            {"type": "compaction", "timestamp": "T2"},

   120→
   121→    def test_mixed_records_only_eligible_processed(self, tmp_path):
   122→        """Only request records WITH messages_full are counted as turns."""
   123→        records = [

   125→            {"type": "response", "timestamp": "T2"},

   143→
   144→class TestSingleTurn:
   145→    def test_no_eviction_default_threshold(self, tmp_path):
   146→        """One API call with 2 user turns and default age_threshold=4
   147→        produces no evictions (max turns_from_end = 1 < 4)."""

   169→    def test_small_results_never_evicted(self, tmp_path):

   171→        # 4 user turns with age_threshold=2: turn 1 has tfend=3 (string, skip),

   188→    def test_error_results_never_evicted(self, tmp_path):

   211→
   212→def _multi_turn_request_1() -> dict:
   213→    """First API call: 4 user turns. With age_threshold=2, the tool_result
   214→    in user turn 2 (tu_1) is old enough to be evicted (turns_from_end=2)."""
   215→    messages = [

   231→def _multi_turn_request_2() -> dict:
   232→    """Second API call: 6 user turns. tu_1 was evicted in request 1.
   233→    Now tu_2 (user turn 4, turns_from_end=2) also gets evicted."""
   234→    messages = [
   235→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   236→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   237→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   238→        ]},
   239→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   240→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   241→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   242→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   243→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   244→        ]},
   245→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   246→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "ok"}]},
   247→        {"role": "user", "content": "do stuff"},
   248→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [

   250→        ]},
   251→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_5", BIG)]},
   252→    ]
   253→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:01:00Z")

   255→
   256→class TestMultiTurnEviction:
   257→    def test_first_request_evicts_oldest(self, tmp_path):
   258→        """Request 1: 4 user turns, threshold=2, tu_1 evicted."""
   259→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "multi.jsonl", [_multi_turn_request_1()])
   260→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   261→
   262→        assert s.total_turns == 1
   263→        assert s.total_evictions == 1
   264→        t = s.turns[0]
   265→        assert t.evictions == 1
   266→        assert t.bytes_saved > 0
   267→
   268→    def test_cumulative_evictions_across_requests(self, tmp_path):

   274→        assert s.total_turns == 2

   276→        assert s.turns[0].evictions == 1

   291→    def test_reduction_pct(self, tmp_path):

   302→    def test_apply_evictions_replaces_previously_evicted(self, tmp_path):

   342→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   343→# (5) Page fault detection when model re-requests evicted content
   344→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   345→
   346→def _fault_request_2() -> dict:
   347→    """Second request that includes a re-read of /foo.py (tu_3) after
   348→    tu_1 (Read /foo.py) was evicted in request 1. tu_3 is a page fault."""
   349→    messages = [
   350→        {"role": "user", "content": "read foo"},
   351→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   352→            _tool_use("tu_1", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   353→        ]},
   354→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_1", BIG)]},
   355→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "got it"}]},
   356→        {"role": "user", "content": "read bar"},
   357→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   358→            _tool_use("tu_2", "Read", {"file_path": "/bar.py"})
   359→        ]},
   360→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_2", BIG)]},
   361→        # The model re-requests /foo.py (page fault)
   362→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   363→            _tool_use("tu_3", "Read", {"file_path": "/foo.py"})
   364→        ]},
   365→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_3", "re-read content")]},
   366→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "done"}]},
   367→        {"role": "user", "content": "continue"},
   368→        {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   369→            _tool_use("tu_6", "Grep", {"pattern": "TODO", "path": "."})
   370→        ]},
   371→        {"role": "user", "content": [_tool_result("tu_6", BIG)]},
   372→    ]
   373→    return _req(messages, ts="2026-03-01T12:01:00Z")
   374→
   375→
   376→class TestPageFaultDetection:
   377→    def test_fault_detected(self, tmp_path):
   378→        """A re-read of /foo.py after tu_1 eviction is a page fault."""
   379→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   380→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "fault.jsonl", records)
   381→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   382→
   383→        # Request 1: tu_1 evicted, no faults.
   384→        assert s.turns[0].faults == 0
   385→        # Request 2: tu_3 re-reads /foo.py → fault detected.
   386→        assert s.turns[1].faults == 1
   387→        assert s.total_faults == 1
   388→
   389→    def test_fault_rate(self, tmp_path):
   390→        """fault_rate = total_faults / total_evictions."""
   391→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   392→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "faultrate.jsonl", records)
   393→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   394→
   395→        assert s.total_evictions > 0
   396→        expected = s.total_faults / s.total_evictions
   397→        assert abs(s.fault_rate - expected) < 1e-9
   398→
   399→    def test_cumulative_faults(self, tmp_path):
   400→        """cumulative_faults tracks running total across turns."""
   401→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()]
   402→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "cumfault.jsonl", records)
   403→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   404→
   405→        assert s.turns[0].cumulative_faults == 0
   406→        assert s.turns[1].cumulative_faults == 1
   407→
   408→    def test_original_tool_use_not_counted_as_fault(self, tmp_path):
   409→        """The original tool_use (tu_1) that produced the evicted result
   410→        should NOT be counted as a fault even though it matches."""
   411→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1()]
   412→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "nofault.jsonl", records)
   413→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   414→
   415→        # tu_1 is in the assistant message AND was evicted, but it's the
   416→        # original call — not a re-request. No fault.
   417→        assert s.total_faults == 0
   418→
   419→    def test_no_double_counting_across_requests(self, tmp_path):
   420→        """The same fault tool_use_id appearing in multiple request records
   421→        should only be counted once."""
   422→        req1 = _multi_turn_request_1()
   423→        req2 = _fault_request_2()
   424→        # Request 3 is a superset of request 2 (session has grown).
   425→        # tu_3 (the fault) is still in the messages.
   426→        msgs3 = json.loads(json.dumps(req2["messages_full"]))
   427→        msgs3.extend([
   428→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "more"}]},
   429→            {"role": "user", "content": "keep going"},
   430→        ])
   431→        req3 = _req(msgs3, ts="2026-03-01T12:02:00Z")
   432→
   433→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "dedup.jsonl", [req1, req2, req3])
   434→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   435→
   436→        # tu_3 appears in both req2 and req3, but only counted once.
   437→        assert s.total_faults == 1
   438→

   665→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   666→# (8) Aggregate stats across multiple sessions
   667→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   668→
   669→class TestAggregateStats:
   670→    def _make_two_sessions(self, tmp_path):
   671→        """Create two log files with known eviction/fault patterns."""
   672→        # Session A: 1 eviction, 0 faults
   673→        p_a = _write_jsonl(
   674→            tmp_path, "a.jsonl", [_multi_turn_request_1()],
   675→        )
   676→        # Session B: 2 evictions, 1 fault (re-read of /foo.py)
   677→        p_b = _write_jsonl(
   678→            tmp_path, "b.jsonl",
   679→            [_multi_turn_request_1(), _fault_request_2()],
   680→        )
   681→        return p_a, p_b
   682→
   683→    def test_aggregate_totals(self, tmp_path):
   684→        """Aggregate totals equal the sum of per-session values."""
   685→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   686→        s_a = replay_session(p_a, age_threshold=2)
   687→        s_b = replay_session(p_b, age_threshold=2)
   688→        sessions = [s_a, s_b]
   689→
   690→        total_turns = sum(s.total_turns for s in sessions)
   691→        total_evictions = sum(s.total_evictions for s in sessions)
   692→        total_bytes_saved = sum(s.total_bytes_saved for s in sessions)
   693→        total_bytes_original = sum(s.total_bytes_original for s in sessions)
   694→        total_faults = sum(s.total_faults for s in sessions)
   695→
   696→        assert total_turns == s_a.total_turns + s_b.total_turns
   697→        assert total_evictions == s_a.total_evictions + s_b.total_evictions
   698→        assert total_bytes_saved == s_a.total_bytes_saved + s_b.total_bytes_saved
   699→        assert total_bytes_original == (
   700→            s_a.total_bytes_original + s_b.total_bytes_original
   701→        )
   702→        assert total_faults == s_a.total_faults + s_b.total_faults
   703→
   704→    def test_aggregate_fault_rate(self, tmp_path):
   705→        """Aggregate fault_rate = total_faults / total_evictions."""
   706→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   707→        s_a = replay_session(p_a, age_threshold=2)
   708→        s_b = replay_session(p_b, age_threshold=2)
   709→
   710→        total_e = s_a.total_evictions + s_b.total_evictions
   711→        total_f = s_a.total_faults + s_b.total_faults
   712→        expected_rate = total_f / total_e if total_e > 0 else 0.0
   713→
   714→        assert total_e > 0
   715→        assert abs(expected_rate - total_f / total_e) < 1e-9
   716→
   717→    def test_aggregate_json_output(self, tmp_path, capsys, monkeypatch):
   718→        """--json with multiple logs produces per-session + aggregate lines."""
   719→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   720→
   721→        monkeypatch.setattr(
   722→            sys, "argv",
   723→            ["replay", "--json", "--age-threshold", "2", str(p_a), str(p_b)],
   724→        )
   725→        main()
   726→
   727→        lines = capsys.readouterr().out.strip().split("\n")
   728→        assert len(lines) == 3
   729→
   730→        sa = json.loads(lines[0])
   731→        sb = json.loads(lines[1])
   732→        agg = json.loads(lines[2])
   733→
   734→        assert agg["type"] == "aggregate"
   735→        assert agg["sessions"] == 2
   736→        assert agg["total_evictions"] == sa["total_evictions"] + sb["total_evictions"]
   737→        assert agg["total_bytes_saved"] == (
   738→            sa["total_bytes_saved"] + sb["total_bytes_saved"]
   739→        )
   740→        assert agg["total_faults"] == sa["total_faults"] + sb["total_faults"]
   741→        assert agg["total_bytes_original"] == (
   742→            sa["total_bytes_original"] + sb["total_bytes_original"]
   743→        )
   744→        # Derived fields
   745→        assert "fault_rate" in agg
   746→        assert "reduction_pct" in agg
   747→
   748→    def test_print_aggregate_no_crash(self, tmp_path, capsys):
   749→        """print_aggregate runs without error on valid sessions."""
   750→        p_a, p_b = self._make_two_sessions(tmp_path)
   751→        sessions = [
   752→            replay_session(p_a, age_threshold=2),
   753→            replay_session(p_b, age_threshold=2),
   754→        ]
   755→        # Should not raise
   756→        print_aggregate(sessions)
   757→        out = capsys.readouterr().out
   758→        assert "AGGREGATE" in out
   759→
   760→    def test_print_aggregate_empty(self, capsys):
   761→        """print_aggregate with no sessions produces no output."""
   762→        print_aggregate([])
   763→        assert capsys.readouterr().out == ""
   764→
   765→    def test_print_session_report_no_crash(self, tmp_path, capsys):
   766→        """print_session_report runs cleanly on a session with turns."""
   767→        records = [_multi_turn_request_1(), _multi_turn_request_2()]
   768→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "report.jsonl", records)
   769→        s = replay_session(p, age_threshold=2)
   770→        print_session_report(s)
   771→        out = capsys.readouterr().out
   772→        assert "Replay:" in out
   773→        assert "Per-turn:" in out
   774→
   775→    def test_print_session_report_empty_session(self, tmp_path, capsys):
   776→        """print_session_report on a session with no turns is safe."""
   777→        p = _write_jsonl(tmp_path, "empty.jsonl", [])
   778→        s = replay_session(p)
   779→        print_session_report(s)
   780→        out = capsys.readouterr().out
   781→        assert "Replay:" in out
   782→        # Should NOT have per-turn output
   783→        assert "Per-turn:" not in out
   784→
   785→
   786→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   787→# Edge cases and _apply_evictions unit test
   788→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   789→
   790→class TestApplyEvictions:
   791→    def test_replaces_evicted_tool_result(self):
   792→        """Directly test _apply_evictions replaces known evicted content."""
   793→        store = PageStore()
   794→        entry = PageEntry(
   795→            tool_use_id="tu_99",
   796→            tool_name="Read",
   797→            tool_input={"file_path": "/evicted.py"},
   798→            original_content="original data here",
   799→            original_size=100,
   800→            summary="[Paged out]",
   801→            evicted_at=0.0,
   802→            turn_index=1,
   803→            turns_from_end=5,
   804→        )
   805→        store.pages["tu_99"] = entry
   806→
   807→        messages = [
   808→            {"role": "user", "content": [
   809→                {"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_99",
   810→                 "content": "original data here"},
   811→            ]},
   812→        ]
   813→        _apply_evictions(messages, store)
   814→
   815→        block = messages[0]["content"][0]
   816→        assert block["content"] == "[Paged out]"
   817→        assert block["tool_use_id"] == "tu_99"
   818→
   819→    def test_skips_non_user_messages(self):
   820→        """_apply_evictions only touches user messages."""
   821→        store = PageStore()
   822→        store.pages["tu_1"] = PageEntry(
   823→            tool_use_id="tu_1", tool_name="Read",
   824→            tool_input={}, original_content="x",
   825→            original_size=1, summary="[paged]",
   826→            evicted_at=0.0, turn_index=1, turns_from_end=5,
   827→        )
   828→
   829→        messages = [
   830→            {"role": "assistant", "content": [
   831→                {"type": "tool_use", "id": "tu_1", "name": "Read",
   832→                 "input": {"file_path": "/f.py"}},
   833→            ]},
   834→        ]
   835→        _apply_evictions(messages, store)
   836→        # Assistant message should be unchanged
   837→        assert messages[0]["content"][0]["type"] == "tool_use"
   838→
   839→    def test_skips_string_content(self):
   840→        """User messages with string content (not list) are skipped."""
   841→        store = PageStore()
   842→        messages = [
   843→            {"role": "user", "content": "just a string"},
   844→        ]
   845→        _apply_evictions(messages, store)
   846→        assert messages[0]["content"] == "just a string"
   847→
   848→    def test_leaves_non_evicted_alone(self):
   849→        """Tool results not in the page store are left unchanged."""
   850→        store = PageStore()
   851→        messages = [
   852→            {"role": "user", "content": [
   853→                {"type": "tool_result", "tool_use_id": "tu_unknown",
   854→                 "content": "keep me"},
   855→            ]},
   856→        ]
   857→        _apply_evictions(messages, store)
   858→        assert messages[0]["content"][0]["content"] == "keep me"
   859→
   860→
   861→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   862→# SessionReplay dataclass properties
   863→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   864→
   865→class TestSessionReplayProperties:
   866→    def test_fault_rate_zero_evictions(self):
   867→        s = SessionReplay(log_path="x")
   868→        assert s.fault_rate == 0.0
   869→
   870→    def test_fault_rate_with_evictions(self):
   871→        s = SessionReplay(log_path="x", total_evictions=10, total_faults=3)
   872→        assert s.fault_rate == pytest.approx(0.3)
   873→
   874→    def test_reduction_pct_zero_bytes(self):
   875→        s = SessionReplay(log_path="x")
   876→        assert s.reduction_pct == 0.0
   877→
   878→    def test_reduction_pct_with_bytes(self):
   879→        s = SessionReplay(
   880→            log_path="x",
   881→            total_bytes_original=1000,
   882→            total_bytes_saved=250,
   883→        )
   884→        assert s.reduction_pct == pytest.approx(25.0)
